text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST
SURVEILLANCE STUDY

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Renata Okonkwo is a 40-year-old female with a history of Stage IIB triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma of the left breast (ypT1b ypN1 M0, RCB class II after neoadjuvant chemotherapy), status post bilateral nipple-sparing mastectomy with left axillary lymph node dissection and subsequent stage 2 implant-based reconstruction. She carries a confirmed germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (c.5074del) and underwent risk-reducing bilateral laparoscopic salpingo-oophorectomy. She has completed all planned systemic therapy including neoadjuvant pembrolizumab plus dose-dense doxorubicin-cyclophosphamide followed by weekly paclitaxel plus pembrolizumab, and adjuvant capecitabine (8 cycles) plus pembrolizumab (17 total doses). The final cycle of pembrolizumab was complicated by immune-related pneumonitis (Grade 2) that was treated with corticosteroids and has since resolved clinically. This is a routine 12-month post-surgical surveillance study performed approximately 6 months following completion of all systemic therapy.

COMPARISON:
Prior CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed approximately 6 months earlier, at the midpoint of adjuvant therapy.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed in the portal venous phase following administration of 90 mL of iohexol IV contrast without complication. Axial images were obtained at 1.25 mm slice thickness with multiplanar reconstructions performed in coronal and sagittal planes. Lung windows, soft tissue windows, and bone windows were reviewed.

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Breast/Chest Wall: The patient is status post bilateral nipple-sparing mastectomy. Bilateral silicone implants are in place, appearing symmetric and without evidence of implant rupture, capsular contracture, or periprosthetic fluid collection. The implant shells demonstrate intact margins bilaterally. No discrete soft tissue mass is identified along the anterior chest wall, overlying skin, or in the subcutaneous tissues adjacent to either implant. No evidence of chest wall recurrence. The pectoralis major and minor muscles are intact bilaterally without mass or infiltration.

Axilla: The left axilla demonstrates expected post-surgical changes with fatty replacement and architectural distortion at the prior dissection bed. No enlarged or morphologically suspicious lymph nodes are identified in the left axilla. The largest visible left axillary soft tissue structure measures approximately 0.7 cm and is fatty in appearance, consistent with a residual lymph node or post-operative fat lobule. The right axilla is unremarkable with no enlarged lymph nodes.

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. The previously described bilateral ground-glass opacities in the lower lobes, attributed to pembrolizumab-related immune pneumonitis, have completely resolved with no residual opacity, scarring, fibrosis, or consolidation identified. No new nodules, masses, or areas of consolidation. No endobronchial lesion. The major fissures are intact. The trachea and central airways are patent and without narrowing. No air trapping appreciated on available images.

Pleura: No pleural effusion. No pleural thickening or nodularity. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinal contour is normal. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal mass. The thymus is not distinctly visualized, expected in this age group. No enlarged mediastinal lymph nodes. The subcarinal space is unremarkable. No hilar lymphadenopathy. The mediastinal vessels are patent and without filling defect.

Pericardium: No pericardial effusion. Normal cardiac silhouette.

Bones (thoracic): The visualized thoracic spine, ribs, sternum, and shoulder girdles demonstrate normal cortical and trabecular architecture. No lytic or sclerotic lesions concerning for osseous metastatic disease. No pathologic fracture. No periosteal reaction.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and morphology, measuring approximately 14.5 cm in craniocaudal span on scout images. The hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously on portal venous phase imaging without focal hypodense or hyperdense lesion. No hepatic mass or nodule identified. The hepatic veins and portal vein are patent. No biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is present and unremarkable, without gallstones or wall thickening.

Spleen: The spleen is normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No splenic lesion. No splenomegaly.

Pancreas: The pancreas enhances uniformly without ductal dilatation or peripancreatic soft tissue stranding. No pancreatic mass or cystic lesion.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are of normal size and configuration. No adrenal nodule or mass. No adrenal enlargement.

Kidneys and Ureters: The kidneys are symmetric in size and show prompt and symmetric cortical enhancement. No renal cortical mass or cyst above the threshold for characterization. No hydronephrosis. No perinephric stranding. The ureters are not dilated.

Retroperitoneum and Mesentery: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymph node enlargement. The aorta is normal in caliber and without aneurysmal dilatation. The inferior vena cava is patent. No soft tissue mass in the retroperitoneal space.

Bowel: The visualized loops of small and large bowel are of normal caliber without focal wall thickening, obstruction, or pneumatosis. No free intraperitoneal air.

Peritoneum and Ascites: No ascites. No peritoneal nodularity or implants to suggest peritoneal carcinomatosis.

Abdominal Wall: No hernia or soft tissue abnormality.

Bones (abdominal): No suspicious lytic or blastic lesion in the lumbar spine, pelvis, or proximal femora. Vertebral body heights are maintained throughout.

PELVIS:

Gynecologic Structures: The patient is status post bilateral laparoscopic salpingo-oophorectomy. The adnexal regions are surgically absent. No residual ovarian or adnexal soft tissue mass is identified. Post-surgical clips are noted bilaterally in the adnexal beds, consistent with prior laparoscopic ligation sites. The uterus is present and unremarkable in size and contour, without evidence of endometrial thickening or myometrial mass on the available CT images. No free pelvic fluid.

Lymph Nodes (pelvic and inguinal): No enlarged pelvic, external iliac, obturator, or inguinal lymph nodes. All visible lymph nodes are within normal size limits.

Bladder: The bladder is partially distended and unremarkable without wall thickening or intraluminal mass.

Bones (pelvic): The pelvic bones and sacrum demonstrate normal trabecular pattern. No destructive osseous lesion. The hip joints appear unremarkable.

Soft Tissues: No suspicious soft tissue mass in the pelvis or perineum.

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of locoregional recurrence. The bilateral mastectomy sites and left axillary dissection bed demonstrate expected post-operative changes without identifiable soft tissue mass or lymphadenopathy to suggest disease recurrence. Bilateral silicone implants appear intact and well-positioned.

2. No pulmonary nodules or masses. Complete resolution of the bilateral lower lobe ground-glass opacities previously attributed to pembrolizumab-induced immune pneumonitis, with no residual parenchymal abnormality.

3. No hepatic lesions, adrenal abnormalities, or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy to suggest abdominal or hepatic metastatic disease.

4. Post-surgical absence of bilateral adnexal structures following laparoscopic salpingo-oophorectomy, without evidence of peritoneal implants or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

5. No suspicious osseous lesion in the visualized axial or appendicular skeleton.

Overall, this surveillance examination is reassuring and demonstrates no imaging evidence of recurrent or metastatic breast carcinoma at this 12-month post-surgical interval.

Report verified and electronically signed by Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging Section.
